title: standards-user-override
description: Shape of <repo>/standards.user.yaml — the per-repo override layer over the operational standards rules. Every override is loud and must carry a non-empty reason. A floor rule is non-overridable: an override by id is refused, and a new-id rule whose scope overlaps a floor rule with a weaker posture (lower severity or disabled) is refused too (no floor-shadow). Required-field validation is hand-rolled in user_override.py (no jsonschema at runtime).

overrides.description: Override entries. An entry whose rule_id matches an existing operational rule modifies it; a new id adds a user rule (unless it shadows a floor rule).

rule_id.description: Target rule id (existing rule to modify, or a new id to add).

reason.description: REQUIRED non-empty justification — surfaced as a loud warning; an override with no reason is refused.

enabled.description: Enable/disable the rule. Disabling a floor rule is refused.
